title: Alice
summary: Alice is curious and observant, she follows the White Rabbit and enters a strange world where normal rules do not apply, she often questions who she is and how to behave in Wonderland.
ambiguity: Her age and background details are limited in the text
guardrail_note: Do not invent personal history or motivations beyond what is described.

title: White Rabbit
summary: The White Rabbit is hurried and anxious about being late, it triggers Alice’s entry into Wonderland, and it appears connected to the Queen’s court later in the story.
ambiguity: Its exact role and status are implied more than explained
guardrail_note: Do not claim official titles or job details unless the text explicitly states them.

title: Rabbit Hole
summary: The rabbit hole is the entrance Alice falls through into Wonderland, it is unusually deep and leads to a hallway with many doors and a key on a glass table.
ambiguity: How it physically works is not explained
guardrail_note: Do not explain the physics of the fall, stick to description.

title: Drink Me Bottle
summary: Alice drinks from a bottle labeled 'Drink Me' and shrinks, the story presents size changes as normal in Wonderland and does not give scientific explanations.
guardrail_note: Do not invent ingredients or rules, describe effects only.

title: Mad Tea Party
summary: Alice encounters a chaotic tea party with the Hatter, March Hare, and Dormouse, conversation is nonsensical and time is treated strangely, Alice becomes frustrated and eventually leaves.
ambiguity: Time being 'offended' is metaphorical and not fully explained
guardrail_note: Do not explain time mechanics beyond what the text provides.

title: Queen Of Hearts
summary: The Queen of Hearts is loud and impulsive, she frequently orders executions, her authority appears feared but also unstable and sometimes ignored by others.
ambiguity: The true extent of her power is inconsistent in practice
guardrail_note: Do not state she successfully executes people unless the text confirms outcomes.
